questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: In the context of the evolution of Indian cinema and its recognition, which of the following distinguishes the Dadasaheb Phalke Award from other National Film Awards like 'Best Feature Film'?

options.text: It is the only award in the ceremony that carries a 'Swarna Kamal' (Golden Lotus).
It is awarded for overall contribution to the growth of Indian cinema rather than for a specific film.
It is the only award presented by the President of India.
It is the only award that includes a cash prize along with a certificate.

questions.explanation: Unlike specific category awards (like Best Feature Film or Best Actor) which recognize excellence in a particular work, the Dadasaheb Phalke Award is given for 'outstanding contribution to the growth and development of Indian cinema' as a whole. Other awards also carry the Swarna Kamal and cash prizes, and the President presents the National Film Awards generally.
questions.takeaway: The Dadasaheb Phalke Award is a lifetime achievement honour for systemic contribution to the industry.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: It is awarded for “its outstanding contribution to the growth and development of Indian cinema.
